title: Using Global Collaboration to Solve Asia's Energy Crisis
gist: An ASPI/WEF blog excerpt details how the 2026 Middle East conflict triggered Asia's worst energy supply shock in decades, adding ~$3.36bn/month to ASEAN's energy import bill and cascading into fertilizer, naphtha, sulfur and EV-battery input shortages across South Asia and the region's manufacturing economies.

insights.claim: The 2026 Middle East conflict has produced Asia's most significant energy supply shock in decades, adding an estimated $3.36 billion to the monthly energy import bill for ASEAN alone and triggering cascading secondary crises.
insights.speaker: Asia Society Policy Institute
insights.so_what: Quantifies the macro hit to ASEAN economies; energy-import-dependent Asian currencies and trade balances are under pressure.
insights.quote: This is adding an estimated $3.36 billion to the energy import bill every month for ASEAN alone
insights.locator: Since conflict erupted in the Middle East

insights.claim: Asia drives 60% of global growth, accounts for more than half of global manufacturing output, and has become a leading center of innovation, making its energy vulnerability a systemic global risk.
insights.speaker: Asia Society Policy Institute
insights.so_what: Frames why an Asian energy shock transmits globally; Asia's growth weighting amplifies the spillover.
insights.quote: The region drives 60% of global growth, accounts for more than half of global manufacturing output

insights.claim: Fertilizer disruption is as consequential as oil for Asia: within weeks of the conflict's escalation India reportedly agreed to urea imports at prices significantly above pre-conflict levels, feeding food inflation, rupee depreciation and a rising import bill.
insights.speaker: Asia Society Policy Institute
insights.so_what: Watch urea/fertilizer prices and Indian rupee as transmission channels of the energy shock into food inflation.
insights.quote: India, for example, reportedly agreed to urea imports at prices significantly above pre-conflict levels
insights.locator: South Asia sits at the centre

insights.claim: South Asia sits at the center of vulnerability because of deep dependence on energy and fertilizer imports from the Middle East.
insights.speaker: Asia Society Policy Institute
insights.so_what: South Asian import-dependent economies are the most exposed to sustained Middle East commodity disruption.
insights.quote: South Asia sits at the centre of this vulnerability because of its deep dependence on energy and fertilizer imports from the Middle East.
insights.locator: South Asia sits at the centre

insights.claim: Strait of Hormuz trade disruptions threaten naphtha supply to Japan, South Korea and Indonesia, raising costs that filter into packaging, consumer goods and broader manufacturing supply chains.
insights.speaker: Asia Society Policy Institute
insights.so_what: Naphtha-dependent petrochemical and plastics producers in North/Southeast Asia face margin compression; watch Hormuz shipping.
insights.quote: Japan, South Korea and Indonesia rely heavily on imported naphtha, a key input for plastic production
insights.locator: Trade disruptions through the Strait of Hormuz

insights.claim: Sulfur shortages are creating challenges for Indonesia's nickel processing capacity, impacting stainless steel and EV battery production.
insights.speaker: Asia Society Policy Institute
insights.so_what: A non-obvious second-order link: a Middle East energy shock hits the EV-battery supply chain via Indonesian nickel processing.
insights.quote: Sulfur shortages are also creating challenges for Indonesia’s nickel processing capacity and impacting stainless steel and electric vehicle (EV) battery production.

insights.claim: The crisis underscores a recurring pattern in Asia's response to energy and commodity shocks and points to the need for Asian countries to fundamentally rethink their energy systems.
insights.speaker: Asia Society Policy Institute
insights.so_what: Repeated shocks build the structural case for accelerated Asian energy diversification and renewables investment.
insights.quote: This crisis underscores a recurring pattern in Asia’s response to energy and commodities shocks
